title: Build a Gemini App
description: Capstone: build an AI chat app with the Gemini API
lesson:
Building with the Gemini API

The Gemini API (Google AI SDK for Android) lets you add generative AI features to your app. Key capabilities:

  • Text generation — single-turn and multi-turn conversations
  • Streaming — token-by-token responses for real-time UI updates
  • Multimodal — combine text with images for vision tasks
  • Structured output — JSON mode for reliable parsing

Setting Up the Gemini API in Android

Step 1: Dependencies

// build.gradle.kts (app module) dependencies { implementation("com.google.ai.client.generativeai:generativeai:0.9.0") // Or the newer google-ai-client SDK: // implementation("com.google.ai.client:google-ai-client:1.0.0") }

Step 2: API Key Management

Never hardcode API keys in source code. They will end up in version control and your APK (which anyone can decompile). Use local.properties + BuildConfig, or better yet, proxy through your own backend server for production apps.
// local.properties (git-ignored) GEMINI_API_KEY=AIzaSy... // build.gradle.kts — expose via BuildConfig android { defaultConfig { buildConfigField( "String", "GEMINI_API_KEY", "\"${project.findProperty("GEMINI_API_KEY")}\"" ) } buildFeatures { buildConfig = true } } // Access in code: val apiKey = BuildConfig.GEMINI_API_KEY

GenerativeModel Configuration

The GenerativeModel is the entry point for all Gemini interactions. Configure it once and reuse.

val generativeModel = GenerativeModel( modelName = "gemini-2.0-flash", // fast + cheap for most tasks apiKey = BuildConfig.GEMINI_API_KEY, generationConfig = generationConfig { temperature = 0.7f // 0=deterministic, 1=creative topK = 40 // consider top 40 tokens topP = 0.95f // nucleus sampling threshold maxOutputTokens = 2048 }, safetySettings = listOf( SafetySetting(HarmCategory.HARASSMENT, BlockThreshold.MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE), SafetySetting(HarmCategory.HATE_SPEECH, BlockThreshold.MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE), SafetySetting(HarmCategory.SEXUALLY_EXPLICIT, BlockThreshold.ONLY_HIGH), SafetySetting(HarmCategory.DANGEROUS_CONTENT, BlockThreshold.MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE) ), systemInstruction = content { text("You are a helpful Android development tutor. " + "Give concise answers with Kotlin code examples.") } )
Model selection guide:
- gemini-2.0-flash: Fast, cheap, good for chat and simple tasks. Best default choice.
- gemini-1.5-pro: More capable for complex reasoning, long context (up to 1M tokens).
- gemini-1.5-flash: Balance of speed and capability.
- Use flash models for real-time chat; use pro models for analysis tasks.

Text Generation

Simple Single-Turn Prompt

// Basic text generation — single request/response suspend fun generateResponse(prompt: String): String { val response = generativeModel.generateContent(prompt) return response.text ?: "No response generated" } // With system instructions for context suspend fun explainCode(code: String): String { val prompt = "Explain this Kotlin code in simple terms:\n```\n$code\n```" return generativeModel.generateContent(prompt).text ?: "" }

Streaming Responses

Streaming shows text as it generates, creating a typing effect. Much better UX than waiting 2-5 seconds for a complete response.

// Streaming — token-by-token delivery via Flow suspend fun generateStreaming(prompt: String, onChunk: (String) -> Unit) { var fullResponse = "" generativeModel.generateContentStream(prompt).collect { chunk -> val text = chunk.text ?: "" fullResponse += text onChunk(fullResponse) // update UI with accumulated text } } // In ViewModel — streaming to UI state fun sendMessage(prompt: String) { viewModelScope.launch { _uiState.update { it.copy(isGenerating = true) } // Add placeholder message for streaming val messageId = addMessage(Role.MODEL, "") var accumulated = "" generativeModel.generateContentStream(prompt).collect { chunk -> accumulated += chunk.text ?: "" updateMessage(messageId, accumulated) // typing effect } _uiState.update { it.copy(isGenerating = false) } } }

Multi-Turn Chat

The ChatSession maintains conversation history automatically. Each message includes full context of previous turns.

// Start a chat session (maintains history) val chat = generativeModel.startChat( history = listOf( content(role = "user") { text("Hi, I'm learning Android") }, content(role = "model") { text("Welcome! I can help with that.") } ) ) // Send messages — context is maintained automatically suspend fun chat(message: String): String { val response = chat.sendMessage(message) return response.text ?: "" } // Streaming chat suspend fun chatStreaming(message: String, onChunk: (String) -> Unit) { var full = "" chat.sendMessageStream(message).collect { chunk -> full += chunk.text ?: "" onChunk(full) } } // Access conversation history val conversationHistory: List<Content> = chat.history
Context window limit: When the total tokens (all history + new message + expected response) exceed the model's limit, the API returns an error. You must manage history yourself — either trim old messages, summarize earlier conversation, or use a sliding window of the last N messages.
// History management — sliding window to stay within token limits class ManagedChat( private val model: GenerativeModel, private val maxHistorySize: Int = 20 // keep last 20 messages ) { private val history = mutableListOf<Content>() suspend fun send(message: String): String { // Trim history if too long if (history.size > maxHistorySize) { history.subList(0, history.size - maxHistorySize).clear() } val chat = model.startChat(history = history.toList()) val response = chat.sendMessage(message) // Update history from the chat session history.clear() history.addAll(chat.history) return response.text ?: "" } }

Multimodal Input — Images + Text

Gemini can process images alongside text prompts. Use the content { } builder to combine inputs.

// Send image + text for analysis suspend fun analyzeImage(bitmap: Bitmap, prompt: String): String { val content = content { image(bitmap) // add the image text(prompt) // add the text prompt } val response = generativeModel.generateContent(content) return response.text ?: "No analysis available" } // Example uses: val description = analyzeImage(photoBitmap, "Describe this image in detail") val calories = analyzeImage(foodPhoto, "Estimate the calories in this meal") val code = analyzeImage(screenshotBitmap, "Convert this UI screenshot to Compose code") // Multiple images in one request suspend fun compareImages(img1: Bitmap, img2: Bitmap): String { val content = content { image(img1) image(img2) text("Compare these two images and describe the differences") } return generativeModel.generateContent(content).text ?: "" } // IMPORTANT: Resize large images before sending to reduce latency fun Bitmap.resizeForGemini(maxDim: Int = 1024): Bitmap { val ratio = minOf(maxDim.toFloat() / width, maxDim.toFloat() / height) if (ratio >= 1f) return this return Bitmap.createScaledBitmap( this, (width * ratio).toInt(), (height * ratio).toInt(), true ) }

Building a Chat UI in Compose

// Chat message data class data class ChatMessage( val id: String = UUID.randomUUID().toString(), val text: String, val role: Role, // USER or MODEL val timestamp: Long = System.currentTimeMillis(), val isStreaming: Boolean = false ) // UI State data class ChatUiState( val messages: List<ChatMessage> = emptyList(), val isGenerating: Boolean = false, val error: String? = null ) // Chat screen composable @Composable fun ChatScreen(viewModel: ChatViewModel) { val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsState() val listState = rememberLazyListState() // Auto-scroll to bottom when new message arrives LaunchedEffect(uiState.messages.size) { if (uiState.messages.isNotEmpty()) { listState.animateScrollToItem(0) // reversed layout } } Column(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize()) { LazyColumn( state = listState, modifier = Modifier.weight(1f), reverseLayout = true // newest at bottom ) { items( items = uiState.messages.reversed(), key = { it.id } // stable keys for performance ) { message -> ChatBubble( text = message.text, isUser = message.role == Role.USER, isStreaming = message.isStreaming ) } } // Error banner uiState.error?.let { error -> Text(error, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error, modifier = Modifier.padding(8.dp)) } // Input bar ChatInput( onSend = { viewModel.sendMessage(it) }, enabled = !uiState.isGenerating ) } } @Composable fun ChatBubble(text: String, isUser: Boolean, isStreaming: Boolean) { Row( modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().padding(8.dp), horizontalArrangement = if (isUser) Arrangement.End else Arrangement.Start ) { Surface( shape = RoundedCornerShape(16.dp), color = if (isUser) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surfaceVariant ) { Text( text = text, modifier = Modifier.padding(12.dp), color = if (isUser) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onPrimary else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant ) } } }

Full Architecture: ViewModel + Repository

Production apps should separate the Gemini API interaction from the ViewModel. This enables testing and swapping models.

┌─────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ │ ChatScreen │────▶│ ChatViewModel │────▶│ AiRepository │ │ (Compose) │◀────│ UiState + │◀────│ │ │ │ │ sendMessage() │ │ generateText() │ └─────────────────┘ └──────────────────┘ │ streamText() │ │ analyzeImage() │ └────────┬─────────┘ │ ┌────────▼─────────┐ │ GenerativeModel │ │ (Gemini SDK) │ └──────────────────┘
// Repository — wraps Gemini SDK with error handling class AiRepository(private val model: GenerativeModel) { private var chat: Chat? = null fun startNewChat(systemPrompt: String? = null) { val history = systemPrompt?.let { listOf(content(role = "user") { text(it) }, content(role = "model") { text("Understood.") }) } ?: emptyList() chat = model.startChat(history = history) } fun streamResponse(message: String): Flow<String> = flow { val activeChat = chat ?: throw IllegalStateException("Call startNewChat first") var accumulated = "" activeChat.sendMessageStream(message).collect { chunk -> accumulated += chunk.text ?: "" emit(accumulated) } } } // ViewModel — manages UI state class ChatViewModel( private val repository: AiRepository ) : ViewModel() { private val _uiState = MutableStateFlow(ChatUiState()) val uiState: StateFlow<ChatUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow() init { repository.startNewChat( systemPrompt = "You are a helpful Android tutor." ) } fun sendMessage(text: String) { // Add user message val userMsg = ChatMessage(text = text, role = Role.USER) _uiState.update { it.copy( messages = it.messages + userMsg, isGenerating = true, error = null )} // Add placeholder for AI response val aiMsgId = UUID.randomUUID().toString() val aiMsg = ChatMessage(id = aiMsgId, text = "", role = Role.MODEL, isStreaming = true) _uiState.update { it.copy(messages = it.messages + aiMsg) } // Stream the response viewModelScope.launch { try { repository.streamResponse(text).collect { partialText -> _uiState.update { state -> state.copy(messages = state.messages.map { msg -> if (msg.id == aiMsgId) msg.copy( text = partialText, isStreaming = true ) else msg }) } } // Mark streaming complete _uiState.update { state -> state.copy( isGenerating = false, messages = state.messages.map { msg -> if (msg.id == aiMsgId) msg.copy(isStreaming = false) else msg } ) } } catch (e: Exception) { _uiState.update { it.copy( isGenerating = false, error = "Error: ${e.message}" )} } } } }

Rate Limiting & Cost Considerations

Gemini API free tier limits (as of 2025):
- gemini-2.0-flash: 15 RPM (requests per minute), 1M TPM (tokens per minute)
- gemini-1.5-pro: 2 RPM, 32K TPM
- Production apps should proxy through your backend to control costs and enforce user-level rate limits.
// Client-side rate limiting class RateLimiter( private val maxRequests: Int = 10, private val windowMs: Long = 60_000 // 1 minute ) { private val timestamps = ArrayDeque<Long>() fun canMakeRequest(): Boolean { val now = System.currentTimeMillis() // Remove timestamps outside the window while (timestamps.isNotEmpty() && timestamps.first() < now - windowMs) { timestamps.removeFirst() } return timestamps.size < maxRequests } fun recordRequest() { timestamps.addLast(System.currentTimeMillis()) } } // Cost estimation helper fun estimateTokens(text: String): Int { // Rough estimate: ~4 chars per token for English return (text.length / 4.0).toInt().coerceAtLeast(1) }
Production architecture: For production apps, do NOT call the Gemini API directly from the client. Instead, proxy through your own backend server. This lets you: (1) Keep the API key server-side, (2) Enforce per-user rate limits, (3) Log and monitor usage, (4) Add content moderation before/after the model, (5) Switch models without an app update.

Structured Output — JSON Mode

For parsing AI responses programmatically, use JSON mode to get reliable structured output instead of free-form text.

// Configure model for JSON output val jsonModel = GenerativeModel( modelName = "gemini-2.0-flash", apiKey = BuildConfig.GEMINI_API_KEY, generationConfig = generationConfig { responseMimeType = "application/json" // force JSON output } ) // Define your expected structure @Serializable data class RecipeAnalysis( val name: String, val calories: Int, val ingredients: List<String>, val difficulty: String // "easy", "medium", "hard" ) // Request structured data suspend fun analyzeRecipe(description: String): RecipeAnalysis { val prompt = """ Analyze this recipe and return JSON with these fields: - name (string) - calories (integer, estimated total) - ingredients (array of strings) - difficulty ("easy", "medium", or "hard") Recipe: $description """.trimIndent() val response = jsonModel.generateContent(prompt) val json = response.text ?: throw Exception("Empty response") return Json.decodeFromString<RecipeAnalysis>(json) } // With schema hint for more reliable output suspend fun extractEntities(text: String): String { val prompt = """ Extract named entities from this text. Return JSON: {"people": [...], "places": [...], "dates": [...]} Text: $text """.trimIndent() return jsonModel.generateContent(prompt).text ?: "{}" }
Tips for reliable structured output:
1. Use responseMimeType = "application/json" — the model will only output valid JSON.
2. Describe the exact schema in your prompt (field names, types, allowed values).
3. Always wrap JSON parsing in try/catch — the model might not match your schema exactly.
4. Use kotlinx.serialization with lenient mode for more forgiving parsing.

Error Handling Best Practices

The Gemini API can fail in several ways. Handle each appropriately.

// Comprehensive error handling sealed class AiResult<out T> { data class Success<T>(val data: T) : AiResult<T>() data class Error(val message: String, val isRetryable: Boolean) : AiResult<Nothing>() } suspend fun safeGenerate( model: GenerativeModel, prompt: String ): AiResult<String> { return try { val response = model.generateContent(prompt) // Check for safety filter blocks val candidate = response.candidates.firstOrNull() if (candidate?.finishReason == FinishReason.SAFETY) { return AiResult.Error( "Response blocked by safety filters", isRetryable = false ) } val text = response.text if (text.isNullOrBlank()) { AiResult.Error("Empty response from model", isRetryable = true) } else { AiResult.Success(text) } } catch (e: com.google.ai.client.generativeai.type.GoogleGenerativeAIException) { AiResult.Error("API error: ${e.message}", isRetryable = true) } catch (e: java.io.IOException) { AiResult.Error("Network error: check your connection", isRetryable = true) } catch (e: Exception) { AiResult.Error("Unexpected error: ${e.message}", isRetryable = false) } }

questions.title: Gemini API Setup
questions.description: Fill in the blanks to initialize the Gemini generative model.
questions.explanation: GenerativeModel is the entry point. Specify model name, API key (via BuildConfig), and generation parameters. temperature controls randomness. API key should NEVER be hardcoded.
hints: The class name describes what it creates

questions.title: Streaming Response
questions.description: What does this code do and why use generateContentStream?
options: Streams the AI response token-by-token, updating the UI progressively — typing effect
questions.explanation: generateContentStream() returns a Flow emitting chunks as they're generated. Each chunk is appended and the UI updates, creating a smooth typing effect. Much better UX than waiting for the entire response.

questions.title: Chat List Performance
questions.description: This chat screen becomes laggy with many messages during streaming. Optimize it.

checks.message: Add key parameter for stable identity

questions.explanation: key = { it.id } gives stable identity so LazyColumn skips unchanged messages during streaming. derivedStateOf for enabled prevents ChatInput recomposition on every chunk.
hints: Without keys, every item recomposes when the list changes

questions.title: Error Handling
questions.description: Write a function that calls Gemini API with proper error handling. Handle network errors, API errors, and empty responses.
questions.explanation: Network calls need: try/catch for exceptions, null/empty check on response, and a sealed result type forcing callers to handle both cases.
hints: Think about all the ways an API call can fail

questions.title: Multi-Turn Chat
questions.description: Fill in the blanks to set up a multi-turn conversation with chat history.

blanks.hint: Sends a message in the chat context

questions.explanation: startChat() creates a ChatSession that maintains conversation history. sendMessage() sends a message with full context of previous turns. history returns all previous Content objects. The model sees the full conversation, enabling contextual responses.
hints: The chat object maintains state across messages
Each new message includes all previous context automatically

questions.title: Image Input (Multimodal)
questions.description: Write a function that sends an image along with a text prompt to the Gemini model for multimodal analysis (e.g., "Describe this image").

checks.message: Include the image in the content
Call generateContent with the built content

questions.explanation: The Gemini API supports multimodal input using the content { } builder. image(bitmap) adds a Bitmap, and text(prompt) adds the text prompt. The model processes both together. Use a vision-capable model like "gemini-pro-vision" or "gemini-1.5-pro". In production, resize large images before sending to reduce latency.
hints: The content builder lets you combine different input types
Both text and image go into a single content block

questions.title: Token Limits
questions.description: What happens when a conversation exceeds the model's context window (token limit)?
options: The model automatically summarizes older messages
The API returns an error — you must manage conversation history yourself
The model silently drops old messages
The response quality improves with more context
questions.explanation: When the total tokens (prompt + history + response) exceed the model's context window, the API returns an error. You must manage conversation length — either by limiting history, summarizing older messages, or using a sliding window. For Gemini Pro, the context window is typically 32K-128K tokens depending on the version. Always track token usage and truncate history proactively.
hints: The model doesn't automatically manage history length
Think about what happens when you keep adding messages forever
